items.title: Cancer Monoclonal Antibodies Market to Grow at a 18.4% CAGR through 2031 | SkyQuest Technology
items.author: Globe Newswire
items.description: The cancer monoclonal antibodies market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 18.4% to reach $337.92 billion by 2031, driven by the high incidence of cancer and advancements in biotechnology. The rising use of combination therapies and biosimilars is also expected to create new opportunities for companies in this market.

items.title: CD40 Targeted Therapies Clinical Trials & Market Opportunity Report 2025,  Insight By Company, Country, Indication & Phase
items.author: Researchandmarkets.Com
items.description: CD40 targeted therapies represent a promising and rapidly developing area of research across various diseases, with several candidates in late-stage clinical trials. The market is expected to grow as these therapies potentially become a vital treatment option in the coming years.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Amgen is mentioned as one of the notable players entering the competitive landscape of CD40 targeted therapies, but no specific details about their involvement are provided.
Sanofi's anti-CD40 antibody, Frexalimab, is currently in phase 3 trials for autoimmune conditions, indicating its advanced stage of development.

GenMab's DuoBody platform, which enables the creation of bispecific antibodies, has been leveraged to develop a promising CD40 targeted therapy, GEN1042/BNT312, currently in phase 2 trial for malignant solid tumors.
BioNTech is collaborating with GenMab on the development of the CD40 targeted therapy GEN1042/BNT312, indicating their involvement in this promising area of research.
Biogen is mentioned as one of the notable players entering the competitive landscape of CD40 targeted therapies, but no specific details about their involvement are provided.

items.title: 2 No-Brainer Dividend Stocks With Yields Over 3% to Buy in 2025 and Hold for a Decade or Longer
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: The article recommends two dividend-paying stocks, Coca-Cola and Amgen, as attractive long-term investments due to their consistent dividend growth and strong product portfolios.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights Coca-Cola's consistent dividend growth, strong brand recognition, and ability to offset declining soda consumption with new product offerings, making it a promising long-term investment.

The article notes Amgen's long history of leading the biotechnology industry, its growing product portfolio, and the potential for continued dividend growth, making it an attractive investment option.
